sections.paragraph:
Ch. pron. 2 peps. sing. m., thou, Dan 2:29, 31 , 37 , 38 3:10 5:13 , 18 , 22 , 23 6:17 , 21 , in כתיב. The form is a Hebraism, peculiar to the biblical Chaldee, instead of the common אַנְתְּ, אַתְּ comm. gen., and thus it is not acknowledged by the Masorites, who mark the ה as redundant, and always substitute the אַנְתְּ קרי.

[“אַנְתּוּן Ch. ye, pron. 2 pers. pl. Dan 2:8. In Targg. written אַתּוּן.”]
